summary:
Spacing between maxillary incisors at age 11 is often benign and self-resolving due to ongoing dental development. Initial management should focus on observation rather than active correction, making no treatment the most appropriate first step.

The summary must only contain two sentences strictly related to the core clinical point(s).

Spacing between maxillary incisors in an 11-year-old is commonly incidental and likely resolves during continued skeletal and dental maturation. Therefore, no immediate treatment is indicated, supporting option C based on standard pediatric orthodontic principles.
